data.body:
PASS-1 review [G1G2 — edit-set / scope accuracy] for the follow-up plan "Move .bridge_state/ + reconciler lock files off the shared tickets branch".

AFFIRMATIONS:
- No hallucinated edit targets. All three files in **Files** exist and are correctly characterized at a high level: init.py (`_GITIGNORE` L37, `_GITATTRIBUTES` L49-57, `_commit_gitignore`/`_commit_gitattributes`/`_ensure_merge_ours_driver`); _advisory_lock.py (`_LOCK_FILE` L61, `_GATE_FILE` L62, written via `_write_file_to_tickets_branch` L589-616); push.py (the `_DIRTY_WD` stash→merge→pop dance L158-180).
- High blast radius is acknowledged ("the reconciler currently coordinates cross-clone partly VIA these shared-branch files … needs its own design pass") — under G1G2, acknowledged blast radius is not a fail.

FINDINGS (mis-attributed edit target + un-enumerated consumers — structured payload returned to the harness):
- [ ] Correct the bindings-write attribution and add `reconcile.py` to **Files**: the plan says _advisory_lock.py "writes lock/gate + bindings to the shared branch", but _advisory_lock.py writes ONLY the `.reconciler-*` lock/gate files. `.bridge_state/bindings.json` (+ bindings-retired.json) is committed by reconcile.py::_commit_binding_store_snapshot (`_rel_files` L237; commit block L1197-1210), which exists specifically so bindings survive `git merge origin/tickets` between passes — the durability mechanism the move must replace. (criteria G1G2; evidence reconcile.py:237,1197-1210; _advisory_lock.py:61-62)
- [ ] Enumerate `src/rebar/_store/sync.py` as an edit/verify target: AC#2 ("union merge … conflict-free on all paths") is implemented in sync.py::_do_reconverge/_union_merge (L88-108), not push.py; sync.py L19-20,90-91 explicitly depend on the `merge=ours` .gitattributes that AC#3 removes. (criteria G1G2; evidence sync.py:19-20,88-108)
- [ ] Enumerate the on-disk READERS of bindings.json: resolver.py:50 and binding_store.py:77,80,110 (the last bakes the path into a user-facing recovery hint `git show tickets:.tickets-tracker/.bridge_state/bindings.json`). The plan is silent on whether the worktree path changes; if it does, these break. (criteria G1G2; evidence resolver.py:50; binding_store.py:77-80,110)
- [ ] Enumerate external `git show tickets:.bridge_state/...` consumers that assume the file is committed: scripts/retarget_jira_project.py L105-138 and scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:258 / e2e_validation_probe.sh restore bindings via `git show tickets:.bridge_state/bindings.json` — gitignoring it off the branch makes those lookups fail. (criteria G1G2; evidence retarget_jira_project.py:105-138; e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:258)
- [ ] Name the test artifacts the change must update so AC#3/#4 are reachable: tests/interfaces/store/test_gitattributes_merge.py:50-51 asserts `.bridge_state/* merge=ours` + `.reconciler-* merge=ours` are committed; test_binding_commit_survives_merge.py (L127,174), test_binding_retired_commit.py, test_corrupt_state_guard.py assert bindings.json is committed-to/restorable-from the tickets branch — enumerate kept vs. redesigned. (criteria G1G2; evidence test_gitattributes_merge.py:50-51; test_binding_commit_survives_merge.py:127,174)
